title: Dim-er Dalna (Bengali Egg Curry)

subs.name: firm paneer cubes (200 g) for a paneer dalna
subs.notes: do not score paneer; fry 2 minutes per side only

subs.name: parboiled cauliflower florets
subs.notes: reduce the simmering time to 6 minutes

subs.name: any neutral cooking oil
subs.notes: the Bengali character comes largely from the mustard oil

steps.text: Score the surface of each peeled egg with 3 shallow cuts and rub all over with a pinch of turmeric and salt. This helps the skin colour evenly and the curry sauce cling to the eggs.

Heat mustard oil in a kadai over high flame until pale. Shallow-fry the eggs, rolling them, until golden-brown on all sides, about 4 minutes. Fry the potato cubes in the same oil until lightly golden, 3 minutes. Set both aside.

Add the bay leaf and cumin seeds to the remaining oil over medium heat. Let them crackle for 20 seconds, then add the onions. Cook until the onions turn golden-brown, stirring frequently, about 8 minutes.

Add ginger-garlic paste and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes until the raw smell disappears. Add the tomato with a pinch of salt and cook until it breaks down and oil separates from the masala, about 4 minutes.
Lower the flame. Add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder, stir for 30 seconds to toast the spices without burning. Add the fried potato cubes and coat in the masala.
Pour in 300 ml water, season with salt and bring to a boil. Add the fried eggs, cover and simmer on medium for 10 minutes until the potatoes are fully cooked and the gravy coats a spoon lightly.
Sprinkle garam masala, stir gently and switch off. Let the dim-er dalna rest 5 minutes — it improves noticeably. Serve over steamed rice or with luchi.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → firm paneer cubes (200 g) for a paneer dalna (do not score paneer; fry 2 minutes per side only); mustard-oil → any neutral cooking oil (the Bengali character comes largely from the mustard oil); potato → parboiled cauliflower florets (reduce the simmering time to 6 minutes)
